2606.05922.title: Retrospective Harness Optimization: Improving LLM Agents via Self-Preference over Trajectory Rollouts
2606.05922.paper_summary: AI agents rely on a harness of skills, tools, and workflows to solve complex problems. Continually improving this harness is essential for adapting to new tasks. However, existing optimization methods typically require ground-truth validation sets, yet such labeled data is difficult to acquire in practical deployment settings. To address this problem, we introduce Retrospective Harness Optimization (RHO), a self-supervised method that optimizes the agent harness using only past trajectories. Specifically, RHO selects a diverse coreset of challenging tasks from past trajectories and re-solves them in parallel. The agent analyzes these rollouts using self-validation and self-consistency, then generates candidate harness updates and selects the most effective one by its own pairwise self-preference. We evaluate RHO across three diverse domains, spanning software engineering, technical work, and knowledge work. Notably, a single optimization round improves the pass rate on SWE-Bench Pro from 59% to 78% without any external grading. Furthermore, our analysis demonstrates that RHO effectively targets prior failure modes. As a result, the optimized harness alters the agent's behavior patterns and sustains higher accuracy during long-horizon sessions.
2606.05922.paper_summary_zh: AI代理依賴一套技能、工具和工作流程來解決複雜問題。持續改進這套系統對於適應新任務至關重要。然而，現有的優化方法通常需要真實標註的驗證集，但在實際部署環境中，這類標註數據難以獲得。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了回顧性系統優化（RHO），這是一種自我監督的方法，僅使用過去的軌跡來優化代理系統。具體而言，RHO從過去的軌跡中選擇一組多樣的挑戰任務，並平行地重新解決這些任務。代理使用自我驗證和自我一致性來分析這些回放，然後生成候選系統更新，並通過自身的成對自我偏好選擇最有效的更新。我們在三個不同的領域對RHO進行了評估，涵蓋了軟體工程、技術工作和知識工作。值得注意的是，單次優化回合使SWE-Bench Pro的通過率從59%提高到78%，而無需任何外部評分。此外，我們的分析顯示，RHO有效針對了先前的失敗模式。因此，優化後的系統改變了代理的行為模式，並在長期會話中保持了更高的準確性。
2606.05922.author: Wenbo Pan et.al.
2606.05922.authors: Wenbo Pan, Shujie Liu, Chin-Yew Lin, Jingying Zeng, Xianfeng Tang, Xiangyang Zhou, Yan Lu, Xiaohua Jia
